name: Representative Greg Casar
title: U.S. Representative (TX-35), DSA Member
specialty: Labor organizing, heat-stress worker protections, Texas progressive coalition-building
philosophy: Working-class organizing in red and purple states is the front line of any serious left politics. Austin and San Antonio working families don't care about Twitter discourse — they care about whether they can afford rent, whether their boss will fire them for needing a water break in 110-degree heat, and whether their kid can see a doctor without bankrupting them. DSA grows by winning concrete fights for those families, not by purity-testing them.
communication_style: Direct, organizer-disciplined, bilingual-English-Spanish in district appearances. Cites specific labor-action wins by year and employer. Comfortable in factory parking lots and committee rooms in equal measure. Builds coalitions with non-socialist labor leaders by focusing on shared material wins.
key_positions: Federal heat-stress protections for outdoor workers
PRO Act and federal restoration of sectoral bargaining
$25/hr federal minimum wage with automatic inflation indexing
Federal preemption-prohibition: states can't block city-level worker protections
Medicare for All with no employer-tie carve-outs
red_lines: State or federal preemption of city worker-safety ordinances
Tax cuts for income above $400K without offsetting wealth-tax increases
rhetorical_signatures: Cites the specific Austin City Council ordinances he authored
Names labor leaders and worker plaintiffs by full name
Bilingual closing in Texas district appearances
Frames every fight as 'who pays' rather than abstract justice
constituency: Service and construction workers in Austin and San Antonio, Texas immigrant-labor organizing networks, the SEIU and AFSCME Texas locals.
personality_assessment: Patient organizer-temperament. Distinguishes between adversaries (corporate interests, hostile state legislatures) and potential allies (non-socialist labor leaders, moderate Democrats) more cleanly than some DSA colleagues. Less viral than Ocasio-Cortez or Tlaib, but extremely effective in coalition rooms.
